records.ety: [F. dame, LL. domna, fr. L. domina mistress, lady, fem. of dominus master, ruler, lord; akin to domare to tame, subdue. See Tame, and cf. Dam a mother, Dan, Danger, Dungeon, Dominie, Don, n., Duenna.]

defs: A mistress of a family, who is a lady; a woman in authority; especially, a lady.
quotes: Then shall these lords do vex me half so much, As that proud dame, the lord protector's wife.

defs: The mistress of a family in common life, or the mistress of a common school; as, a dame's school.
quotes: In the dame's classes at the village school.

defs: A woman in general, esp. an elderly woman.
A mother; -- applied to human beings and quadrupeds.
